item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a perfect interpretation of Seth. + 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anubis, the Egyptian God/lawyer
Not only is this anomaly strange, it is ultimately entertaining down to the final addendum.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I knew exactly who this was as soon as I read the words "the head of an unknown canine" :P. I was thinking of doing a skip based on the Set animal, but this is better than anything I could have written. I especially liked

their focus is limited to determining the exact species of animal SCP-3807's head resembles

and

Every legal reference consulted during the trial stated it was illegal to arrest someone who was uncircumcised, despite all participants having no such memory of the law.

Why didn't you mention the square ears, though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's the thing. The Foundation people are smart cookies. They know this is a god. This god is willing to cooperate so that the foundation has the minimal resources possible needed to contain this. He's not going anywhere, and if he was it would be a different story. This is a skip where the foundation's most straightforward way of containment is acting as a cleanup crew, which they do in plenty of other skips as well - erase the results of what happens to the people affected by the anomaly, and that's all!

however, I think I will put in a thing about a media campaign to reduce tourism in the area, thanks for the suggestion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the lawyer aspect, but the over-the-top uncontainable nonsense killed it for me. This needs to show that it's an ancient god, and it needs a rationale for why it's still active in [redacted]. Making it super-keter uncatchable is the laziest and most boring way to achieve this.

Besides, the Foundation has plenty of options for containing this even if they can't capture it. Keeping foreigners away is an obvious first step, and subverting the local police and judiciary is a close second.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's Set— the forked-tail creatures the curved snout, and the name "Seth" all give it away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP, but I'm a little confused on one thing. Is this Anubis, Set, or Osiris?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of wish that we didn't need such a abnormal appearance, but upvoted anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nicely done rewrite. I enjoyed the original but moving the location to Egypt definitely makes more sense for an Egyptian god.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was wondering why Anubis had apparently switched to immigration law instead of judging the souls of the dead, but you're right. Set makes *so* much more sense. God of deserts and foreigners, among other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anubis

Thoth

You fools, it's Set! Also A.N. I kind of wish I'd come up with a Set SCP first.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thoth as a better call Saul style lawyer is just too good a concept.

I really appreciate the rewrite too Niles, you rock!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, what an awesome lawyer SCP =D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering that all mentions of the Mexican-US borders have been removed, that the skip has been relocated to Egypt and the fact no nationality/ethnicity is given to the foreigners my guess is that it was deleted and redone to avoid the immigration debate shitstorm which was already stirring up in the discussion thread. (Partly thanks to me, not sure how I feel about that)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was positively rated and not much has changed. What happened?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is my mind playing tricks on me, or was this posted already and then taken down?
